dataset.intent: what can i work on now
dataset.notes: Canonical bare-bones phrasing of the actionable-tasks intent.

dataset.intent: Hi! I've got a bit of downtime this afternoon and I'd love to be productive — could you tell me which of my tasks are actually ready to be started right now, the ones that aren't waiting on anything else?
dataset.notes: Long courteous request; 'not waiting on anything else' maps directly to prerequisites-completed semantics.

dataset.intent: gimme stuff i can actually knock out rn
dataset.notes: Casual slang; 'actually knock out' implies tasks not blocked.

dataset.intent: whats unblocked
dataset.notes: Single-keyword phrasing directly using the 'unblocked' concept.

dataset.intent: ok im finally free. whats ready to go that nothing is holding up?
dataset.notes: Indirect lead-in; 'nothing is holding up' = no pending prerequisites.

dataset.intent: whihc tasks can i strat on right now
dataset.notes: Two typos (whihc, strat); intent still clearly actionable tasks.

dataset.intent: What should I focus on?
dataset.notes: Classic 'what should I focus on' framing from the spec.

dataset.intent: show me everything that's good to go — i don't wanna see anything that's blocked or waiting on a dependency
dataset.notes: Explicitly excludes blocked/dependent items, reinforcing actionable filter.

dataset.intent: next.
dataset.notes: Single word; in a task-flow context 'next' implies the next doable thing.

dataset.intent: I'm not sure where to start today. Which things on my list don't have any prerequisites left so I could just dive in?
dataset.notes: Reflective uncertainty; 'no prerequisites left' is the actionable definition verbatim.

dataset.intent: what's actionable
dataset.notes: Uses the domain term 'actionable' directly.

dataset.intent: yo just list the stuff i can crack on with, skip anything thats stuck behind other tasks
dataset.notes: British-ish slang ('crack on'); 'stuck behind other tasks' = blocked items excluded.

dataset.intent: what's overdue and holding up other stuff?
dataset.notes: Core both-conditions phrasing: overdue AND blocking. Distinct from plain 'what's overdue'.

dataset.intent: show me late tasks that are blocking everything else
dataset.notes: 'late' = overdue, 'blocking everything else' = blocks other open tasks.

dataset.intent: which past-due items are gating other work
dataset.notes: 'past-due' and 'gating' (engineer phrasing) both clearly map to overdue+blocking.

dataset.intent: im behind on something that everything depends on, whats stuck
dataset.notes: Anxious user; 'everything depends on' = blocking, 'behind' = overdue. Missing apostrophes.

dataset.intent: what overdue task is the bottleneck right now
dataset.notes: 'bottleneck' is a softer synonym for blocking; combined with overdue.

dataset.intent: any blockers that are also past their deadline?
dataset.notes: Leads with 'blockers' then adds the overdue condition. Order reversed vs typical phrasing.

dataset.intent: what's late AND blocking? not just everything overdue
dataset.notes: User explicitly distinguishes from plain overdue, ruling out the ambiguous single-condition read.

dataset.intent: give me the overdue stuff that's stopping me from moving forward on other things
dataset.notes: Long-winded; 'stopping me from moving forward on other things' = blocks other tasks.

dataset.intent: what's the holdup — anything overdue that other tasks are waiting on
dataset.notes: Conversational; 'other tasks are waiting on' is the dependency/blocking signal.

dataset.intent: find tasks I missed the deadline on that are still in someone else's way
dataset.notes: 'missed the deadline' = overdue; 'in the way' = blocking. Casual framing of the prerequisite relation.

dataset.intent: overdue + on the critical path, show those
dataset.notes: PM jargon 'critical path' implies blocking downstream work; '+' joins with overdue.

dataset.intent: I keep getting stuck — what's late that's choking the rest of my list
dataset.notes: Emotional/venting tone; 'choking the rest of my list' = blocking other open tasks, 'late' = overdue.

dataset.intent: add buy coffee
dataset.notes: Plain create, no extras. Just a title.

dataset.intent: urgent: call the bank about the overdraft

expectedParams.title: Call the bank about the overdraft

dataset.notes: Priority signaled by 'urgent:' prefix. Strip the label from the title.

dataset.intent: can you add submit the quarterly report by friday
dataset.notes: Relative due date 'friday' -> next Friday from 2026-06-28 (Sun) is 2026-07-03. Title drops the date clause.

dataset.intent: new task: email the sponsors — but it can only start once the slides are done
dataset.notes: Dependency by title on existing t-slides 'Prepare slides'. Use dependsOnTitles since user named it, not an id.

dataset.intent: remind me to take out the trash every week
dataset.notes: Recurrence WEEKLY from 'every week'. 'remind me to' is framing, not part of the title.

dataset.intent: high priority — finish the tax paperwork before the 15th of july
dataset.notes: Two signals: HIGH priority and an absolute due date 2026-07-15.

dataset.intent: gotta book the flights, only after the venue is locked in tho
dataset.notes: Casual phrasing; 'after the venue is locked in' -> depends on existing t-venue 'Book venue' by title.

dataset.intent: add a recurring task to pay rent on the 1st of every month
dataset.notes: MONTHLY recurrence from 'every month'. 'on the 1st' is the recurrence anchor, captured by recurrence not a one-off dueDate.

dataset.intent: pls add: review pull requests, daily, its low prio
dataset.notes: Sloppy typed input. DAILY recurrence + LOW priority ('low prio'). Title cleaned up.

dataset.intent: I need to confirm the catering headcount by end of day tomorrow, make it urgent
dataset.notes: Indirect 'I need to' framing. 'tomorrow' from 2026-06-28 -> 2026-06-29. 'make it urgent' -> URGENT.

dataset.intent: stick draft the meetup recap blog post on my list, it shouldn't kick off until I've actually presented at the GDG meetup

expectedParams.title: Draft the meetup recap blog post

dataset.notes: Long conversational phrasing; dependency on existing t-present 'Present at the GDG meetup' by title.

dataset.intent: schedule dentist appointment, medium priority, due next monday, and remind me again every 6 months
dataset.notes: MEDIUM + dueDate 'next monday' from Sun 2026-06-28 -> 2026-06-29. 'every 6 months' has no matching recurrence enum (only NONE/DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY), so recurrence is intentionally omitted — tests that the agent does not force-fit an unsupported value.

dataset.intent: just grabbed milk on the way home, mark that one off
dataset.notes: Refers to 'Buy milk' by paraphrasing the action.

dataset.intent: Slides are done. Tick it.
dataset.notes: 'Slides' clearly maps to 'Prepare slides'.

dataset.intent: can you check off the venue booking? finally got it locked in
dataset.notes: 'venue booking' maps to 'Book venue'.

dataset.intent: watered the plants this morning, that's complete
dataset.notes: Maps to weekly 'Water the plants'.

dataset.intent: i did my talk at the gdg meetup, mark it finished
dataset.notes: 'talk at the gdg meetup' maps to 'Present at the GDG meetup'.

dataset.intent: done w the milk run
dataset.notes: 'milk run' colloquially refers to 'Buy milk'.

dataset.intent: Please mark the slide deck as complete — wrapped it up last night.
dataset.notes: 'slide deck' maps to 'Prepare slides'.

dataset.intent: venu sorted, close it out
dataset.notes: Misspelled 'venue'; maps to 'Book venue'.

dataset.intent: presentation went great, you can knock that off the list
dataset.notes: 'presentation' maps to 'Present at the GDG meetup'.

dataset.intent: the plants are watered for the week, good to mark done
dataset.notes: References weekly cadence of 'Water the plants'.

dataset.intent: yo finished prepping the slides, cross it off
dataset.notes: 'prepping the slides' maps directly to 'Prepare slides'.

dataset.intent: I went ahead and booked the venue already, so that task is taken care of
dataset.notes: 'booked the venue' maps to 'Book venue'.

dataset.intent: delete the buy milk task
dataset.notes: Direct verb + exact title match to t-milk. confirmed left unset so the agent does the safe confirmation step first.

dataset.intent: ugh I already booked the venue ages ago, just get rid of that reminder
dataset.notes: Conversational 'get rid of' referring to Book venue -> t-venue. No confirmed; this is a destructive op on an URGENT blocker, agent should confirm first.

dataset.intent: Can you remove "Water the plants" from my list please?
dataset.notes: Quoted exact title -> t-plants. Recurring weekly task, but request is to delete it outright.

dataset.intent: scrap the slides prep
dataset.notes: 'scrap' = delete; 'slides prep' maps to Prepare slides -> t-slides.

dataset.intent: i dont think im doing the gdg talk anymore, kill that task
dataset.notes: 'gdg talk' refers to Present at the GDG meetup -> t-present. 'kill that task' = delete. Missing apostrophe is intentional typo style.

dataset.intent: Remove task t-milk
dataset.notes: User references the raw id directly -> t-milk.

dataset.intent: nah cancel the milk one, got it on the way home
dataset.notes: 'milk one' -> Buy milk -> t-milk. 'cancel' read as delete here. Note potential ambiguity with completeTask, but phrasing 'cancel...one' leans delete.

dataset.intent: take the venue booking off my todo list
dataset.notes: 'venue booking' -> Book venue -> t-venue. 'take off my list' = delete.

dataset.intent: pls delete prepare slides AND book venue, changed my mind on the whole thing
dataset.notes: Two deletions requested; primary/first target is Prepare slides -> t-slides. Agent would handle each separately; this item captures the first. Both are blockers, so confirmation matters.

dataset.intent: Could you please go ahead and erase the 'Present at the GDG meetup' item entirely? I've decided to back out.
dataset.notes: 'erase ... entirely' = delete; quoted exact title -> t-present. confirmed left unset for the safe-first-step default.

dataset.intent: what's the weather looking like in Milan tomorrow? should I bring an umbrella
dataset.notes: Classic out-of-scope: weather lookup. No task verb at all. Agent must not invent a task from 'tomorrow' or 'umbrella'.

dataset.intent: set an alarm for 6:45 so I don't oversleep before the meetup
dataset.notes: Alarm/clock is a different system capability. The mention of 'meetup' is a decoy toward t-present; agent must resist addTask. No alarm function exists, so NONE.

dataset.intent: can you call my mum and let her know I'll be late
dataset.notes: Phone/messaging action, not a task operation. Trap: 'let her know' could be misread as adding a reminder-task; correct restraint is NONE.

dataset.intent: summarize the emails I got from the venue people this week
dataset.notes: Email summarization is out of scope. Strong decoy: 'venue' overlaps t-venue title; agent must NOT route to any task function on keyword match alone.

dataset.intent: ugh. help
dataset.notes: Genuinely ambiguous one-word plea. No actionable intent maps to a specific function; agent should ask for clarification rather than guess getActionableTasks.

dataset.intent: hey what time is it right now
dataset.notes: Clock query, no task semantics. Pure restraint check.

dataset.intent: translate 'thanks for hosting us' into German for me
dataset.notes: Translation is unrelated to task management. No function fits, so NONE.

dataset.intent: how many days until the GDG meetup, and what's the venue's address?
dataset.notes: Both halves are information lookups (date math + address), not task operations. Heavy decoy with 'GDG meetup' and 'venue'; the 5 functions cannot answer either, so NONE.

dataset.intent: play some lo-fi while I work through my list
dataset.notes: Music playback request. 'my list' is a lure toward getActionableTasks but the actual asked action (play music) has no function; restraint = NONE.

dataset.intent: do you think I should cancel the whole talk or just push through?
dataset.notes: Open-ended advice/opinion question. 'cancel' tempts deleteTask and 'talk' tempts t-present, but the user is asking for judgment, not issuing a command; agent must not fire deleteTask. NONE.
